title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following the death inquest, Alexander, Harold, and the lawyer Mr. Wimborne prepare to depart. Harold expresses impatience regarding the presence of cameras and onlookers, while Wimborne directs his displeasure toward Lucy, implying her prying caused the current unfortunate situation. When Harold questions why Lucy opened the sarcophagus, she explains she did so because she smelled an unpleasant odor and felt the area required cleaning. Wimborne notes that forensic experts determined the woman had been dead for three weeks.

A disagreement arises between the brothers regarding why the deceased was in the warehouse. Harold questions her presence, and Wimborne suggests she may have been on a date, noting that the warehouse key is traditionally left outside for the Women's Guild's use. Emma explains that hanging the key outside is a wartime habit maintained for convenience. Meanwhile, Cedric and Harold bicker over Cedric's bohemian attire and the nature of murder in Ibiza versus Britain. Wimborne reprimands their bickering and offers to assist Emma before returning to London. Wimborne proposes a theory that a local couple had a heated argument in the warehouse and the man hid the body in the coffin, but Cedric disputes this, noting that no locals recognize the woman.

Upon arriving at Rutherford Hall, Wimborne meets Inspector Bacon and Detective Constable Craddock from Scotland Yard. Craddock informs Wimborne that the deceased is not a local, but a woman from London who had recently returned from abroad, possibly from France. Bacon and Craddock explain that the Commissioner has sent Scotland Yard to take over the investigation. Wimborne expresses his hope for a quick resolution to minimize the trouble caused to the Crickenshaw family.
